annotations.label: Negative number

anchor.snippet: a negative number is the opposite of a positive real number

annotations.note: Mathlib has the `Neg` typeclass (core Lean) and `InvolutiveNeg`, providing the unary `-` operation, but no standalone definition `negative := -x for positive x`.

annotations.label: Negative number (equivalent)

anchor.snippet: a negative number is a real number that is less than zero

annotations.note: The predicate `x < 0` is just the order on a `LinearOrderedField`; no dedicated `IsNegative` definition exists, but `neg_lt_zero` characterizes negativity.

annotations.label: Positive number

anchor.snippet: a number that is greater than zero is called positive

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses positivity directly as `0 < x`; there is no separate `IsPositive` predicate but order infrastructure is fully present.

annotations.label: Sign of a number

anchor.snippet: the negativity or positivity of a number is referred to as its sign

annotations.note: `SignType.sign : α →o SignType` returns the sign of an element in a suitable ordered structure.

annotations.label: Trichotomy of reals

anchor.snippet: Every real number other than zero is either positive or negative

annotations.note: `lt_trichotomy a b : a < b ∨ a = b ∨ b < a` instantiated at `b = 0` gives the article's trichotomy for any linear order, in particular `ℝ`.

annotations.label: Natural numbers and integers

anchor.snippet: The non-negative whole numbers are referred to as natural numbers

annotations.note: `Nat` (core Lean, used throughout Mathlib) is the type of natural numbers; `Int` is the type of integers.

annotations.label: Order on the number line

anchor.snippet: Numbers appearing farther to the right on this line are greater, while numbers appearing farther to the left are lesser

annotations.note: The linear order on ℝ encodes the number-line order; `lt_trichotomy` and `LinearOrder` formalize the informal left/right comparison.

annotations.label: Negative magnitude comparison

anchor.snippet: a negative number with greater magnitude is considered less

annotations.note: `neg_lt_neg_iff : -a < -b ↔ b < a` (and `abs_lt`) formalize that a more-negative number is smaller.

annotations.label: Positive in signed context

anchor.snippet: a number that is greater than zero is referred to as positive

annotations.note: Positivity is encoded as the predicate `0 < x`; Mathlib has no `IsPositive` wrapper but uses this throughout.

annotations.label: Nonnegative and nonpositive

anchor.snippet: the term nonnegative is sometimes used to refer to a number that is either positive or zero

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the raw predicates `0 ≤ a` and `a ≤ 0` rather than named definitions `Nonneg`/`Nonpos`.

annotations.label: Negative as subtraction result

anchor.snippet: Negative numbers can be thought of as resulting from the subtraction of a larger number from a smaller

annotations.note: `sub_neg_of_lt : a < b → a - b < 0` is exactly this characterization.

annotations.label: Subtraction of larger from smaller

anchor.snippet: the subtraction of a larger number from a smaller yields a negative result

annotations.note: `sub_neg_of_lt` states precisely that `a < b` implies `a - b < 0`.

annotations.label: Minus sign operator

anchor.snippet: signifies the operator for both the binary

annotations.note: The `Neg` (unary) and `Sub` (binary) typeclasses (with `-` notation overloaded) capture both meanings of the minus sign.

annotations.label: Unary negation of positive

anchor.snippet: A special case of unary negation occurs when it operates on a positive number

annotations.note: `neg_pos`/`neg_neg_of_pos` express that negating a positive number yields a negative number.

annotations.label: Addition of two negatives

anchor.snippet: Addition of two negative numbers is very similar to addition of two positive numbers

annotations.note: `Left.add_neg`/`Left.add_neg_of_neg_of_nonpos` give that the sum of two negative numbers is negative.

annotations.label: Mixed-sign addition

anchor.snippet: one can think of the negative numbers as positive quantities being subtracted

annotations.note: `sub_eq_add_neg : a - b = a + -b` formalizes treating addition with negatives as subtraction.

annotations.label: Subtraction as adding negative

anchor.snippet: subtraction of a positive number yields the same result as the addition of a negative number of equal magnitude

annotations.note: `SubNegMonoid.sub_eq_add_neg` (and its theorem form `sub_eq_add_neg`) is exactly this identity.

annotations.label: Subtracting a negative

anchor.snippet: subtracting a negative number yields the same result as the addition a positive number of equal magnitude

annotations.note: `sub_neg_eq_add : a - -b = a + b` formalizes this.

annotations.label: Sign rules of multiplication

anchor.snippet: The product of one positive number and one negative number is negative

annotations.note: `mul_neg_of_pos_of_neg`, `mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos`, `mul_pos_of_neg_of_neg`, and `mul_pos` collectively encode all sign rules of multiplication.

annotations.label: Sign-of-product rule

anchor.snippet: the sign of any product a × b depends on the sign of a as follows

annotations.note: `mul_pos_iff : 0 < a * b ↔ 0 < a ∧ 0 < b ∨ a < 0 ∧ b < 0` is the iff form of the sign-of-product rule.

annotations.label: Sign rules of division

anchor.snippet: The sign rules for division are the same as for multiplication

annotations.note: `div_pos_of_neg_of_neg`, `div_neg_of_neg_of_pos`, `div_neg_of_pos_of_neg`, and `div_pos` cover the four sign-rule cases for division.

annotations.label: Division sign rule

anchor.snippet: If dividend and divisor have the same sign, the result is positive

annotations.note: `div_pos_iff : 0 < a / b ↔ 0 < a ∧ 0 < b ∨ a < 0 ∧ b < 0` precisely captures the same-sign rule for division.

annotations.label: Negation of a number

anchor.snippet: The negative version of a positive number is referred to as its negation

annotations.note: The `Neg` typeclass and the unary `-` notation are the formalization of negation.

annotations.label: Negation is additive inverse

anchor.snippet: the negation of a positive number is the additive inverse of the number

annotations.note: `neg_add_cancel : -a + a = 0` (in any `AddGroup`) says the negation is the additive inverse.

annotations.label: Negation extended to all reals

anchor.snippet: The negation of 0 is 0, and The negation of a negative number is the corresponding positive number

annotations.note: `neg_zero` and `InvolutiveNeg.neg_neg : - -x = x` give the two stated identities.

annotations.label: Absolute value

anchor.snippet: The absolute value of a number is the non-negative number with the same magnitude

annotations.note: `|a| = a ⊔ -a` is defined for `Lattice`s with `Neg`, with `abs_nonneg` confirming the nonnegativity property.

annotations.label: Integers as ordered pairs

anchor.snippet: by defining integers as an ordered pair of natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib uses core Lean's `Int` (defined via `ofNat`/`negSucc`) rather than the Grothendieck pair construction `ℕ × ℕ / ~`.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation on pairs

anchor.snippet: We define an equivalence relation ~ upon these pairs with the following rule

annotations.note: The specific relation `(a,b) ~ (c,d) ↔ a + d = b + c` for the pair construction of ℤ is not in Mathlib since ℤ is defined differently.

annotations.label: Integers as quotient set

anchor.snippet: we identify two pairs ( a , b ) and ( c , d ) if they are equivalent in the above sense

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Int` is not constructed as a quotient of `ℕ × ℕ`.

annotations.label: Integers form a ring

anchor.snippet: equipped with these operations of addition and multiplication, is a ring

annotations.note: `Int.instCommRing : CommRing ℤ` shows ℤ is a (commutative) ring.

annotations.label: Total order on integers

anchor.snippet: We can also define a total order

annotations.note: The `LinearOrder ℤ` instance gives the total order on integers.

annotations.label: Additive zero, inverse, unit, subtraction

anchor.snippet: an additive zero of the form ( a , a ), an additive inverse of ( a , b ) of the form ( b , a )

annotations.note: The abstract structure (zero, neg, one, sub on ℤ) exists via `CommRing ℤ`, but the specific representatives in the pair construction are not used.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of additive inverse

anchor.snippet: The additive inverse of a number is unique

annotations.note: `neg_eq_of_add_eq_zero_left` and `neg_eq_of_add_eq_zero_right` express uniqueness of the additive inverse in any `AddGroup`.
